advancedVocab.tornati: returned (note: implies having been there before)
advancedVocab.Salite in: get in; hop in (note: informal invitation, often used when inviting someone into a vehicle or to join an experience)

advancedVocab.dedicati a: dedicated to; focused on (note: often used for content/topics)
advancedVocab.iscrivetevi al canale: subscribe to the channel (note: common YouTube phrase)
advancedVocab.viaggiare è la vostra passione: travel is your passion (note: idiomatic way to say 'if you love traveling')

vocab.aver preso possesso: having taken possession; after taking possession (note: idiomatic for 'checked in')

advancedVocab.prendere possesso: to take possession; to move in (context: accommodation)
advancedVocab.cioè: that is; i.e.; namely (discourse marker, clarifying or specifying)

advancedVocab.Allora: So; Well (discourse marker, often used to start a sentence or transition)
advancedVocab.biblioteca: library (can be confused with 'libreria', which means 'bookstore')

vocab.per questo: because of this; for this reason

advancedVocab.Forse è per questo che: Maybe that's why; Perhaps that's why
advancedVocab.biglietti per a gennaio: tickets for January (note: 'per a gennaio' is a nonstandard/colloquial construction)

vocab.facciamo: we do; let's do (note: can mean 'let's' or 'we do/make', depending on context)

advancedVocab.Infatti: in fact; actually (discourse marker, emphasizes explanation or clarification)

vocab.fa: ago; does; makes (here: ago, as in 'time ago')

advancedVocab.giro: walk; stroll; outing (can mean 'walk' or 'outing' depending on context)
advancedVocab.sprizzino: small Spritz; little Spritz (diminutive form, not standard Italian)
advancedVocab.come si deve: properly; as it should be; the right way
advancedVocab.di grandezza decente: of decent size; of reasonable size

vocab.bruttini: a bit ugly; not very nice (diminutive)
vocab.la prima sera: the first evening; the first night

beginnerVocab.bruttini: a bit ugly; not very nice (diminutive of 'brutto')

advancedVocab.bruttini: a bit ugly; not very nice (diminutive, mildly negative)
advancedVocab.piccolini: a bit small; quite small (diminutive, affectionate or minimizing)
advancedVocab.ristorante a tema: theme restaurant (restaurant with a specific theme or decor)

vocab.ce l'abbiamo fatta: we did it; we made it
vocab.vi facciamo vedere: we show you; we'll show you

advancedVocab.Date un'occhiata: Take a look; Have a look (imperative, informal plural)
advancedVocab.cioè: that is; I mean (discourse marker, filler)
advancedVocab.c'è: there is; you know (can be a filler/discourse marker)
advancedVocab.ce l'abbiamo fatta: we made it; we did it (idiomatic expression)

vocab.e basta: and that's it; and that's all
vocab.li trovi benissimo: you find them very easily; you can find them easily

advancedVocab.a base di: based on; featuring (used for food, e.g., meat-based)
advancedVocab.si paga il fatto che: you pay for the fact that; the price reflects that (nuanced: not just the food, but the experience)
advancedVocab.un po' a tema: somewhat themed; a bit themed (refers to a place with a specific decorative or experiential theme)
advancedVocab.e basta fondamentalmente: and that's basically it; and that's fundamentally all (discourse marker, summarizing)
advancedVocab.No perché: No, because (discourse marker, used to introduce a justification or explanation)

advancedVocab.rosticcerie: rotisseries; takeaway delis (Italian fast-food shops specializing in roasted foods)
advancedVocab.prezzi che sono veramente assurdi: prices that are really unbelievable; prices that are truly absurd (emphasizes how cheap or surprising the prices are)
advancedVocab.il posto più economico: the cheapest place (superlative construction, may be confused with 'economic' meaning 'related to economy')

advancedVocab.catena: chain (can mean 'chain store' or 'network' in this context)
advancedVocab.si paga direttamente: you pay directly (impersonal construction; 'one pays directly')

vocab.che dovete vedere: that you must see; that you have to see
vocab.si è: it is; it has (note: part of a reflexive or passive construction)

advancedVocab.assolutamente: absolutely; definitely (used for emphasis, often stronger than in English)
advancedVocab.edificio: building (can refer to a large or important structure, not just any building)

advancedVocab.guadagnato il primato: gained the distinction; achieved the record (note: 'primato' here means 'record' or 'distinction', not 'primacy')
advancedVocab.edifici più pesanti: heaviest buildings (note: 'pesanti' literally means 'heavy', but here refers to massiveness or weight in a structural sense)
advancedVocab.il pentagono: the Pentagon (note: refers to the US Department of Defense headquarters, culturally specific reference)

advancedVocab.neoclassico e comunista: neoclassical and communist (architectural styles; unusual pairing)
advancedVocab.imponente: imposing; impressive (nuanced: conveys grandeur, not just size)

advancedVocab.c'è un bellissimo sole: there's beautiful sun (note: 'sole' as 'sunshine', not just 'sun')
advancedVocab.ha cambiato nome: changed its name (note: 'cambiare nome' as 'to be renamed')
advancedVocab.ci fu: there was (note: literary/past historic form, less common in spoken Italian)

vocab.destituirlo: to depose him; to remove him
vocab.quanta: how much; how many (contextual, may refer to 'how many people' or 'how much' in general)

advancedVocab.mise agli arresti: placed under arrest; put under house arrest (formal/legal phrasing)
advancedVocab.l'allora: the then- (at that time; used to refer to someone's former position)
advancedVocab.destituirlo: to depose him; to remove him from office (formal/political term)
advancedVocab.Praticamente: basically; practically (discourse marker, often used to summarize or emphasize)
advancedVocab.ci fu: there was; there occurred (impersonal construction, often used in narration)

vocab.costa pochissimo: costs very little; is very cheap

advancedVocab.roba: stuff; things (informal, can mean 'things' in a broad sense)
advancedVocab.bolle: bubbles (here: dome-shaped tents or structures, not literal bubbles)
advancedVocab.riscaldate: heated (warmed up; refers to the domes being warm inside)
advancedVocab.gulash: goulash (a traditional Central/Eastern European stew, not originally Italian)

vocab.chiorbo: chiorbo (unclear; possibly a local food or slang)
vocab.vi portiamo: we take you; we bring you

advancedVocab.ciè: there is; there's (colloquial contraction of 'ci è')

advancedVocab.capsa: Capsa (name of a famous historic restaurant in Bucharest)
advancedVocab.da caro cubere: at Caru' cu Bere (name of a famous restaurant; note: 'caro cubere' is a mishearing or misspelling of 'Caru' cu Bere')
advancedVocab.attrazione: attraction (in the sense of a tourist or cultural draw, not just 'attraction' in general)

vocab.ve ne: to you of it; of them to you (ve = to you all, ne = of it/them)

advancedVocab.alla fine del video: at the end of the video
advancedVocab.ve ne faremo vedere un altro: we will show you another one (of them) (note: 've ne' = 'to you of them', a pronominal construction)

vocab.sarebbe: would be; is (in this context, explanatory)

advancedVocab.valida alternativa: valid alternative (can mean 'good option' in context)
advancedVocab.sta dentro dentro: is inside inside (emphatic repetition for emphasis or to mean 'really inside')

advancedVocab.si svolgono: take place; are held (note: reflexive construction for events)
advancedVocab.coinvolgono: involve; engage (note: can mean 'draw in' or 'include')
advancedVocab.serate: evenings; evening events (note: often refers to social events, not just time of day)

vocab.alle terme: to the thermal baths; to the spa
vocab.siamo alle terme: we are at the thermal baths; we are at the spa
vocab.vi facciamo vedere: we show you; we'll show you

advancedVocab.ospiti: guests; visitors (can refer to people being hosted or attending an event)
advancedVocab.terme: thermal baths; hot springs (public spa facilities, culturally common in Italy and parts of Europe)
advancedVocab.raga: guys; folks (slang, short for 'ragazzi', informal address to a group)
advancedVocab.fa un freddo incredibile: it's incredibly cold (idiomatic, emphasizes extreme cold)
advancedVocab.Non avete idea: You have no idea (idiomatic, expresses something is beyond imagination)

advancedVocab.carina: cute; nice (note: can refer to appearance or general pleasantness)
advancedVocab.freddo polare: freezing cold; polar cold (note: idiomatic, means extremely cold)
advancedVocab.un po' complicato: a bit complicated; a little tricky (note: 'un po'' softens the statement)
advancedVocab.filmare: to film; to record (note: less common than 'registrare' for audio, but standard for video)
advancedVocab.non è facile: it's not easy (note: can imply 'it's difficult' in context)
advancedVocab.più Bron: more Bron (note: unclear, possibly a name or reference, not standard Italian)

vocab.se vorreste: if you would like; if you want (polite/conditional)

advancedVocab.Terme: thermal baths; spa (public bath complex, often with pools and saunas)
advancedVocab.aree relax: relaxation areas (dedicated spaces for resting, common in spas)
advancedVocab.fateci sapere: let us know (imperative + pronoun, informal request for feedback)
advancedVocab.interamente dedicato: entirely dedicated (completely focused on, often used for content or events)

advancedVocab.anului manuk: Anului Manuk (proper name; may be unfamiliar)

advancedVocab.ancora ancora e ancora: again and again and again (emphatic repetition, idiomatic)
advancedVocab.nel senso che: meaning that; in the sense that (discourse marker, clarifying)

advancedVocab.a quando entra: when it starts; when it comes in (note: 'a quando' is an unusual construction for 'when', often context-dependent)
advancedVocab.assistere a: to attend; to witness (note: can mean both to be present at and to watch something)
advancedVocab.diventa una cosa pesante: becomes a heavy thing; becomes burdensome (note: 'pesante' is figurative here, meaning emotionally or mentally heavy)

vocab.ci ha lasciati a bocca aperta: left us open-mouthed; amazed (expression of astonishment)

advancedVocab.Milite Ignoto: Unknown Soldier (refers to a national monument for unidentified fallen soldiers)
advancedVocab.ci ha lasciati a bocca aperta: left us speechless; amazed us (idiom: literally 'left us with open mouth')

vocab.ce ne sarebbero: there would be (of them);there would be (here)
vocab.scopritele: discover them;find them out (imperative, you all)

advancedVocab.ce ne sarebbero mille altre: there would be a thousand more (things; idiomatic, emphasizes abundance)
advancedVocab.scopritele: discover them (imperative, referring to things previously mentioned)

advancedVocab.ci vediamo al prossimo viaggio: see you on the next trip (idiomatic farewell for travel content)
